name: Manny Albam
abstract: Manny Albam (June 24, 1922 – October 2, 2001) was an American jazz arranger, composer, record producer, saxophonist, and educator.

interviews.title: Manny Albam interviewed by Monk Rowe, Tarrytown, New York, October 31, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: Eddie Bert
about_quotes.evidence: in that band I met Shorty Rodgers, Stan Getz, Manny Albam, all these guys were playing in the band.
about_quotes.doc_title: Eddie Bert interviewed by Monk Rowe, Danbury, Connecticut, November 20, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Michael Abene
about_quotes.evidence: the late Manny Albam and I initiated at Manhattan School of Music
about_quotes.doc_title: Michael Abene interviewed by Monk Rowe, Toronto, Canada, January 11, 2003

about_quotes.speaker: Terry Gibbs
about_quotes.evidence: I got all the guys that used to be in Woody’s band... Ernie Wilkins writing, Manny Albam writing, Johnny Mandel. ... I gave it to the guys that I thought would do it. ... Manny Albam the Basie things.
about_quotes.doc_title: Terry Gibbs interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 12, 2001 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Kirchner
about_quotes.evidence: In 1989 and ’91 I spent two years studying with Bob Brookmeyer and Manny Albam in the BMI Jazz Composers’ Workshop. And they taught us a technique called “Pitch Modules”...
about_quotes.doc_title: Bill Kirchner interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, June 4, 2021

about_quotes.speaker: Steve Buckingham,
about_quotes.evidence: INTERVIEWER: Yeah. I had a funny, Manny Albam had a funny anecdote about, I think he was producing the Guess Who. [INTERVIEWEE] Who was it? ... [INTERVIEWER] Manny Albam.
about_quotes.doc_title: Steve Buckingham, interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, February 25, 2019 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Mandel Johnny
about_quotes.evidence: Georgie had a very good book, he was fun to work with, he was a great player himself, we had a lot of good charts, Al Cohn, Manny Albam, Bud Johnson, all kinds of people.
about_quotes.doc_title: Mandel Johnny — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
